company.name: Travere Therapeutics
company.subtitle: FILSPARI full FDA approval in FSGS Apr 13 2026 · IgAN commercial · pegtibatinase Phase 3 HARMONY restarted Q1 2026
company.cashRunway: Self-funding (FILSPARI growing 100%+ YoY, $410M FY25 sales, no near-term raise expected)

market.notes: FILSPARI is first non-immunosuppressive IgAN therapy. Q4 2025 ~$103M US sales (+108% YoY), 908 new patient starts in Q4. FY2025 ~$410M total US sales. Patient population ~150K US IgAN, ~30K patients addressable in moderate-severe segment. Competitors: Tarpeyo (Calliditas/Stellantis), Vanrafia (Novartis). Sparsentan dual ETA/AT1 mechanism = unique among orals. Class IIb pricing. Travere expects continued growth as guideline adoption builds.

us.patientsK.note: NKF: ~130K US IgAN prevalent; ~40% progress to ESRD within 20yr; most common primary GN globally.

eu.patientsK.note: ERA registry: ~150K EU27+UK IgAN; Italy/France highest biopsy rates; MEST-C scoring standard.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~1.5M (Asia Pacific 40-50% of primary GN biopsies); Japan screening program drives detection.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- Tarpeyo (budesonide) + Filspari covered post-2023 FDA approvals; Medicare + commercial strong.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- EMA approved Kinpeygo/Filspari; NICE positive; RAASi + SGLT2i baseline therapy.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan JSN guidelines tonsillectomy + steroids; China TCM + supportive care common.

us.priceK.note: Blended $65K: Tarpeyo $15K/9mo course (30%) + Filspari $120K (20%) + RAASi/SGLT2i generic $2K baseline.

eu.priceK.note: ~$32K blended -- Kinpeygo ~$25K course + Filspari ~$70K net + generic backbone.

penPct.note: ~30% peak -- novel agents stacking (Tarpeyo + Filspari + SGLT2i); sibeprenlimab/atrasentan pipeline.

us.reachPct.note: FILSPARI sparsentan IgAN full approval; growing rapidly

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 45% -- established payor adoption + formulary coverage

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 5% of 150K patients; lower than US due to HTA gating + biosimilar pressure

eu.priceK.note: EU net $80K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 0.8% of 1500K -- Japan + select markets; limited ex-Japan access

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 10% -- Japan PMDA reimbursed; emerging markets patchy

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $30K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

assets.name: FILSPARI (sparsentan) — FSGS
assets.stage: Commercial (full approval Apr 13 2026 — first ever FSGS therapy)

market.notes: First and only FDA-approved FSGS therapy as of April 13, 2026. ~40K US FSGS patients, ~30K addressable in non-nephrotic moderate-severe segment. Combined IgAN+FSGS patient pool now >100K US. Sparsentan was already used off-label in FSGS pre-approval — formal label removes payor barriers. PROTECT-style proteinuria reduction data drove approval after FDA extended PDUFA from Jan 13 to Apr 13 2026 for additional clinical benefit characterization. Pediatric label includes patients age 8+.

us.patientsK.note: NKF/NephCure: ~40K US FSGS prevalent; ~5-7K new cases/yr; ~50% progress to ESRD within 5-10yr without remission.

eu.patientsK.note: ERA-EDTA registry: ~45K EU27+UK FSGS; underdiagnosis common; ~7% of primary glomerular disease biopsies.

row.patientsK.note: Global ex-US/EU ~200K (higher APOL1-associated incidence in African ancestry); China/Japan KDIGO-guided.

us.wtpPct.note: ~45% -- Medicare ESRD benefit + commercial cover Filspari (sparsentan); ACTH + CNI supportive still common.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- EMA approved Filspari 2024; NICE HST pathway; RAASi + steroids standard first-line.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- limited novel access; steroids + CNI (tacrolimus/cyclosporine generics) dominate.

us.priceK.note: Blended $90K: Filspari $120K (30% pts) + tacrolimus/CNI generic $3K + ACTH Acthar $200K niche; rituximab off-label $30K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$45K blended -- Filspari ~$70K net + generic CNI dominant backbone.

row.priceK.note: ~$8K -- generic immunosuppressants; novel agents minimal penetration.

penPct.note: ~25% peak -- Filspari + pipeline (atrasentan, obinutuzumab); steroid-resistant segment drives novel uptake.

us.reachPct.note: FILSPARI FSGS full approval Apr 2026; first-ever FSGS therapy

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 38% -- established payor adoption + formulary coverage

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 4% of 45K patients; lower than US due to HTA gating + biosimilar pressure

eu.priceK.note: EU net $80K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 0.6% of 200K -- Japan + select markets; limited ex-Japan access

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 8% -- Japan PMDA reimbursed; emerging markets patchy

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $30K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

assets.name: Pegtibatinase (TVT-058) — Classical Homocystinuria
assets.stage: Phase 3 HARMONY restarted Q1 2026 (~70 patients enrolling)

market.notes: Pegylated cystathionine beta-synthase (CBS) enzyme replacement for classical HCU — genetic CBS deficiency causing toxic homocysteine accumulation, thrombotic events, vision/skeletal abnormalities, developmental delays. ~5-10K diagnosed US patients, no disease-modifying therapy exists. COMPOSE Phase 1/2 OLE: sustained homocysteine and methionine reductions, normalized in one patient. HARMONY Phase 3: 70 pts age 12-65, randomized 1:1 vs placebo, 2.5 mg/kg SC twice weekly, 24-week double-blind. Primary: change in plasma tHcy weeks 6-12. Manufacturing previously delayed program — commercial-scale batches now produced. ENSEMBLE long-term extension also enrolling.

us.patientsK.note: Travere/Orphanet CBS deficiency homocystinuria: ~1.5K US classical homocystinuria (CBS-deficient); ~500 B6-non-responsive pegtibatinase candidates.

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet EU: ~2K classical homocystinuria (Ireland/Qatar highest incidence via newborn screen).

row.patientsK.note: Global homocystinuria ~50K prevalence; ROW treatable ~3K diagnosed.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% -- orphan ultra-rare; Medicare/Medicaid cover betaine (Cystadane) + emerging pegtibatinase (Travere Ph3).

us.priceK.note: $400K/yr anchored to Travere pegtibatinase Ph3 pricing analog (ultra-orphan enzyme therapy); Cystadane ($50K) legacy.

eu.priceK.note: EU net ~$250K post ultra-orphan HTA negotiation.

penPct.note: ~30% peak -- captive ultra-orphan pool, B6-non-responsive subset.

us.reachPct.note: Pegtibatinase classical homocystinuria Phase 3 HARMONY; ultra-rare ~1K US

us.wtpPct.note: US drug-specific WTP 60% -- estimated launch WTP pending Phase 3 + label

eu.reachPct.note: EU reach 45% of 2K; EMA pathway at phase3

eu.wtpPct.note: EU drug-specific WTP 45% -- EMA path pending Phase 3; HTA gating expected

eu.priceK.note: EU net $250K/yr post-HTA (~50-60% of US list typical)

row.reachPct.note: ROW reach 10% of 3K; ex-US/EU access lags by years at phase3

row.wtpPct.note: ROW drug-specific WTP 12% -- ROW access limited to Japan + select APAC at launch

row.priceK.note: ROW blended $100K/yr -- Japan near EU, emerging markets lower via tiered pricing

mega_bear.narrative: FILSPARI growth stalls + pegtibatinase fails Phase 3 — single-product commercial company at premium
events: ✗ FILSPARI growth decelerates as Tarpeyo + Vanrafia take share in IgAN
✗ FSGS launch underwhelms — proteinuria reduction not viewed as meaningful by payors
✗ HARMONY Phase 3 fails to replicate Phase 1/2 homocysteine reductions
→ Re-rates to ~$15 as single-product franchise without pipeline

bear.narrative: FILSPARI grows but FSGS launch slow + HARMONY mixed
events: ~ FILSPARI IgAN reaches ~$700M but plateaus 2028-29
~ FSGS launch: $200-300M peak, slower than IgAN ramp
~ HARMONY: stat sig but modest effect, BLA delayed
→ Stable mid-cap, profitable, pipeline-light

base.narrative: FILSPARI dual-indication franchise builds toward $1B + HARMONY positive
events: ✓ FILSPARI combined IgAN+FSGS franchise reaches $900M-1.1B by 2028
✓ FSGS launch follows IgAN ramp pattern — first-mover advantage
✓ HARMONY Phase 3 hits primary endpoint (homocysteine reduction)
✓ Pegtibatinase BLA filed 2027, launch 2028 — first HCU disease-modifying therapy

bull.narrative: FILSPARI dominant in rare nephrology + pegtibatinase first HCU therapy + M&A talk
events: ✓ FILSPARI franchise $1.3B+ — best-in-class non-immunosuppressive nephrology
✓ Pegtibatinase HARMONY positive — first disease-modifying HCU treatment
✓ Pediatric IgAN/FSGS expansion + ex-US partnerships add upside
✓ M&A target for kidney/rare disease focused buyers (Otsuka, Vertex, BMS)

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Rare nephrology platform — dual blockbuster + pegtibatinase franchise — Otsuka/Astellas acquires at $50+
events: ✓ FILSPARI $1.8B+ peak across IgAN + FSGS globally
✓ Pegtibatinase $400M+ peak as first HCU disease-modifier
✓ Pipeline expansion: additional rare kidney + metabolic indications
✓ Strategic acquirer pays $5-7B for rare disease franchise

lastData.title: FILSPARI full FDA approval in FSGS — first ever therapy for the indication
points: Apr 13 2026: FDA approved FILSPARI for FSGS in adults + pediatric pts age 8+
First and only FDA-approved therapy for FSGS — historic milestone in rare nephrology
Approval expands US addressable population from ~30K (IgAN) to >100K (IgAN+FSGS)
Original PDUFA Jan 13 2026 was extended 3 months for additional benefit characterization
Q4 2025 FILSPARI sales ~$103M (+108% YoY), 908 new patient start forms
$322.8M cash at YE2025, no near-term raise expected
Pegtibatinase HARMONY Phase 3 restarted Q1 2026 after manufacturing optimization

nextCatalyst.title: FSGS launch trajectory + HARMONY enrollment progress
thresholds: Q1 2026 FILSPARI sales — IgAN momentum into FSGS approval
Q2 2026 reported FSGS new patient starts (first quarter post-approval)
HARMONY Phase 3 enrollment update (~70 patients age 12-65)
FILSPARI ex-US milestones (CSL Vifor licensing partner)
Potential label expansion to nephrotic FSGS population

risks: Single-molecule franchise — sparsentan (FILSPARI) is everything until pegtibatinase reads out
FSGS approval was already widely expected — limited upside surprise from approval itself
FSGS is smaller patient population than IgAN — incremental revenue may underwhelm bulls
Tarpeyo (Calliditas) and Vanrafia (Novartis) are active IgAN competitors with growing share
HARMONY restart adds Phase 3 execution risk — manufacturing problems caused prior delay
HCU is small market (~5-10K diagnosed) — even success caps pegtibatinase peak at ~$300-400M
Hepatotoxicity REMS on FILSPARI label limits prescriber base vs unrestricted competitors
No expanded pipeline beyond FILSPARI + pegtibatinase — third asset not visible
Stock at $27.82 / $2.5B mcap = ~6x 2025 sales — premium for single-franchise commercial biotech
FDA extending PDUFA 3 months in Jan 2026 created uncertainty — some bulls had exited

resolved.notes: FDA approved FILSPARI (sparsentan) for FSGS in adults and pediatric patients age 8+ without nephrotic syndrome. First and only FDA-approved therapy for FSGS. Approval came on the extended PDUFA date (originally Jan 13, extended 3 months). Travere held conference call same day at 6:45pm ET. Expands addressable patient population from ~30K IgAN to >100K combined IgAN+FSGS.

catalysts.title: Q2 2026 first quarter of FSGS commercial launch (earnings)
Pegtibatinase HARMONY Phase 3 HCU data readout
catalysts._rationale: Enrollment restarted February 2026, 24-week trial expecting topline data H2 2026
